back_translation: 'You shall make the altar of acacia wood — five cubits long and five cubits wide. The altar shall be square, and three cubits high.'
'Make its horns on its four corners; the horns shall be of one piece with it. Overlay it with bronze.'
'You shall make its pots for removing ashes, its shovels, sprinkling bowls, meat forks, and firepans; all its utensils you shall make of bronze.'
'Make for it a grating of bronze network, and make on the network four bronze rings at its four corners.'
'Set it under the ledge of the altar from below, so that the network reaches halfway up the altar.'
'Make poles for the altar — poles of acacia wood — and overlay them with bronze.'
'The poles shall be inserted into the rings, so that the poles are on the two sides of the altar when it is carried.'
'Make it hollow with boards; as he showed you on the mountain, so they shall make it.'
'You shall make the courtyard of the tabernacle: on the south side, hangings of fine twisted linen — a hundred cubits long for one side.'
'Its twenty pillars and their twenty bases of bronze; the hooks of the pillars and their bands of silver.'
'Likewise on the north side, in length, hangings a hundred cubits long, with its twenty pillars and their twenty bronze bases; the hooks of the pillars and their bands of silver.'
'The width of the courtyard on the west side: hangings of fifty cubits, with their ten pillars and their ten bases.'
'The width of the courtyard on the east side, toward the sunrise, fifty cubits.'
'Fifteen cubits of hangings for the side, with their three pillars and their three bases.'
'On the other side, fifteen cubits of hangings, with their three pillars and their three bases.'
'For the gate of the courtyard, a screen of twenty cubits, of blue and purple and scarlet yarn and fine twisted linen, the work of an embroiderer — with four pillars and four bases.'
'All the pillars of the courtyard round about shall be banded with silver; their hooks of silver, and their bases of bronze.'
'The length of the courtyard one hundred cubits, the width fifty by fifty, the height five cubits — of fine twisted linen, and their bases of bronze.'
'All the utensils of the tabernacle in all its service, and all its pegs and all the pegs of the courtyard — of bronze.'
'You shall command the sons of Israel to bring you pure beaten olive oil for the light, to keep a lamp burning continually.'

'In the Tent of Meeting, outside the veil that is before the Testimony, Aaron and his sons shall tend it from evening until morning before the LORD. It shall be a perpetual statute for their generations on behalf of the sons of Israel.'
